content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 17, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 17, 2026, focused on stabilizing the elizaOS Live USB architecture, hardening Android local-inference paths, and optimizing cloud provisioning workflows. Significant progress was made in cross-platform stability, including refined desktop startup sequences and improved Docker build reliability.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**elizaOS Live USB & OS Infrastructure**
*   Advanced the elizaOS Live USB variant with production-ready update architecture, including signed manifest support and systemd-based health checks ([#7762](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7762)).
*   Hardened the Live USB integration, ensuring branded launcher labels and proper UDisks typelib configuration ([#7757](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7757)).
*   Validated live USB demo paths and root-mode boot configurations ([#7776](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7776)).

**Android & Local Inference Stability**
*   Stabilized Android local-inference activation and native loader integration for stock APKs ([#7769](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7769), [#7771](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7771)).
*   Enabled native TTS playback in TalkMode and improved voice hint forwarding for streaming plugins ([#7763](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7763), [#7767](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7767)).
*   Added long-generation support for Android direct chat paths while maintaining default short-path performance ([#7765](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7765)).

**Cloud & Orchestration Improvements**
*   Folded node health checks into the provisioning-worker daemon and consolidated SSH-bound cron paths ([#7775](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7775), [#7778](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7778)).
*   Consolidated CI workflows by merging `image.yaml` into `build-agent-image.yml` to resolve Docker build failures ([#7780](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7780), [#7777](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7777)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#7783](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7783): Fixes circular symlink issues in the app-core pruner.
*   [#7782](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7782): Introduces connector capability detection and sub-agent UX redesign.
*   [#7779](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7779): WIP on first-party satellites and dynamic view host bridge.
*   [#7774](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7774): Shell foundation v1 and accessibility/contrast improvements.
*   [#7766](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7766): Adds Swift runtime RPC bridge.

## CLOSED ISSUES

**Speculative Decoding & Hardware Validation**
*   Resolved issues regarding M-RoPE position validation and speculative decoding compatibility on Lunar Lake/Vulkan hardware ([#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631)).
*   Closed the investigation into Qwen2.5-14B bandwidth performance on Lunar Lake, determining the observed cliff was load-dependent rather than a persistent kernel regression ([#7638](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7638)).

## NEW ISSUES
*   [#7758](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7758): Integration proposal for the SunfishLoop cross-agent social network.
*   [#7770](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7770): Tracking issue for configuring release credentials for the cross-platform OTA pipeline.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**OpenVINO Runtime RFC ([#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633))**
*   Discussions confirm that the OpenVINO backend is successfully integrated for ASR tasks. Maintainers emphasize that LLM-side routing through OpenVINO is currently discouraged due to performance limitations compared to Vulkan/CUDA. The focus remains on validating ASR performance on Intel NPU hardware and refining the recommender logic to prevent accidental LLM routing to OpenVINO.

**Speculative Decoding SWA Gate ([#7635](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7635))**
*   The issue tracks the silent disabling of speculative decoding for SWA-based models. Recent updates confirm that a fallback patch has been implemented in the `llama.cpp` fork, allowing the system to use checkpoint-mode speculative decoding. Validation is ongoing as maintainers work to verify performance on 27B-class models across different hardware targets.
